title: General Health Department Info
description: Public health services, clinics, and immunizations.
chat_response: I can help guide you to the right health resources.
steps: Check the Health Department website for clinic hours.
Call to make appointments for immunizations.
Review available health forms online.

contacts.name: Health Department
contacts.when_to_use: Use for clinic appointments or public health questions.

title: Department of Social Services (DSS)
description: Assistance with food, energy bills, housing, and other social safety net programs.
chat_response: There are local options for emergency food and help with utility bills, plus longer-term benefits you can apply for.
steps: Apply for benefits via MyMDThink.
Contact DSS for emergency food or cash assistance.
Gather ID, proof of income, and residency documents.

contacts.name: Dept of Social Services
contacts.when_to_use: General info, benefits application, family services

contacts.name: Abuse Reporting Hotline
contacts.when_to_use: Report suspected abuse or neglect

emergency_notice: For immediate child/vulnerable adult abuse danger, call 911.

title: Homeless Services / Emergency Shelter
description: Emergency shelter and homelessness prevention services.
chat_response: You should connect with the local homeless services system to locate shelter or housing assistance.
steps: Call the Homeless Hotline (24/7) for emergency shelter.
Be prepared to share family details for intake.
Call early in the day for better availability.
Ask about prevention programs if not yet homeless.

contacts.name: Homeless Hotline
contacts.when_to_use: Call immediately if you need shelter tonight

title: Police Department (PGPD)
description: Law enforcement services for crimes and public safety.
chat_response: If a crime is happening right now or anyone is in immediate danger, call 911 immediately.
steps: If the crime is in progress, call 911.
For past crimes, file a report online or call non-emergency.
Have incident details ready (date, time, location, description).

contacts.name: Emergency (911)
contacts.when_to_use: Use for any crime happening right now

contacts.name: Non-Emergency Dispatch
contacts.when_to_use: Use for non-urgent police matters

title: Fire & EMS
description: Fire suppression and emergency medical services.
chat_response: If you smell gas, see smoke, or there's a fire, get to safety first and call 911 immediately.
steps: Evacuate immediately if there is fire or gas.
Call 911 from a safe location.
Do not re-enter the building.

contacts.name: Emergency (911)
contacts.when_to_use: Any fire, medical emergency, or gas leak

emergency_notice: Call 911 for fire, medical emergencies, or gas leaks.

title: Mental Health Crisis / Crisis Response
description: Immediate support for mental health crises and emotional distress.
chat_response: I'm really glad you reached out. Support is available, and you don't have to go through this alone.
steps: If immediate danger to life, call 911.
For crisis support, call 988 or the local Mobile Crisis Team.
Stay with the person if safe to do so.
Text 988 if you prefer text over voice.

contacts.name: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
contacts.when_to_use: Call or text if feeling emotionally overwhelmed or in crisis

contacts.name: Local Mobile Crisis Team
contacts.when_to_use: Local mental health crisis team

emergency_notice: Call 911 if there is an immediate threat to life.

title: Foodborne Illness / Food Poisoning
description: Report suspected food poisoning or unsafe food handling.
chat_response: That sounds uncomfortable. Most cases improve, but it's important to watch for warning signs.
steps: Stay hydrated and rest.
Seek medical care if symptoms are severe.
Report suspected outbreaks to Health Dept.

contacts.name: Food Safety Program
contacts.when_to_use: Report suspected food poisoning or food safety violations

emergency_notice: Call 911 if symptoms are severe (dehydration, bloody diarrhea).

title: County 311 / General Services
description: Primary entry point for most non-emergency county services and complaints.
chat_response: Thanks for letting me know. Issues like missed trash or recycling pickup can usually be resolved quickly.
steps: Identify the specific issue.
Visit the PGC311 Customer Portal or call 311.
Search for your issue type and submit a request.
Save your ticket number for follow-up.

contacts.name: Call 311
contacts.when_to_use: Non-emergency county issues: potholes, missed trash, code enforcement

emergency_notice: Use for non-emergency county issues only.

title: Trash, Recycling, or Bulk Pickup Issues
description: Missed trash or recycling collection, bulk item pickup requests.
chat_response: Thanks for letting me know. Issues like missed trash or recycling pickup can usually be resolved quickly.
steps: Confirm your collection day and ensure items are at curb.
Report through County 311 (phone or online).
Provide your address and what was missed.

contacts.name: Department of the Environment
contacts.when_to_use: Report missed collections or request bulk pickup

title: Roads, Potholes, and Streetlight Issues
description: Potholes, damaged roads, malfunctioning streetlights, or traffic signal issues.
chat_response: I can help you report that. Road and streetlight issues are handled by county transportation services.
steps: Note the exact location (address or nearest intersection).
Take photos if possible.
Report through County 311.

contacts.name: Dept of Public Works & Transportation
contacts.when_to_use: Report potholes, streetlights, traffic signals

emergency_notice: If road hazard causes immediate danger, call 911.

title: Noise, Property, or Neighborhood Complaints
description: Noise complaints, abandoned properties, code violations, or neighborhood disturbances.
chat_response: That can definitely be frustrating. The county has a process to handle neighborhood and property-related concerns.
steps: Report the issue through PGC311.
Use non-emergency police for active noise (party, music).
Provide address and description of issue.

contacts.name: DPIE Code Enforcement
contacts.when_to_use: Code enforcement, property maintenance, zoning concerns

contacts.name: Police Non-Emergency
contacts.when_to_use: If noise or disturbance is happening right now

title: Permits, Licenses, and Inspections
description: Building permits, business licenses, inspections, and permit status questions.
chat_response: I can point you to the right place. Permits and inspections are handled by a specific county department.
steps: Check permit requirements online.
Apply via DPIE.
Schedule inspections through the automated system.

contacts.name: DPIE Permits
contacts.when_to_use: Apply for permits, schedule inspections, check status

title: Property Taxes, Billing, and Fees
description: Property tax bills, assessments, and payments.
chat_response: Billing and tax questions are common, and there's a county office that can help clarify this for you.
steps: View and pay bills online.
Contact Finance for assessment questions.

contacts.name: Department of Finance
contacts.when_to_use: Property tax questions, payments, assessments

title: Phishing Email / Government Impersonation
description: Emails pretending to be from a trusted organization to trick you into sharing info.
chat_response: Thanks for checking before clicking. Messages like this are very common scams, and catching it early really matters.
steps: Do not click on any links or attachments.
Do not reply to the email.
Report it using the contacts below.

contacts.name: FTC Report Fraud
contacts.when_to_use: Report any phishing or scam attempt, even if no money was lost

contacts.name: FBI IC3
contacts.when_to_use: Use if you clicked a link, shared credentials, or lost money

emergency_notice: If you shared passwords, change them immediately.

title: Account Compromise / Hacked Account
description: Unauthorized access to your online accounts.
chat_response: That's scary to deal with, but acting quickly can stop things from getting worse.
steps: Change your password immediately.
Turn on multi-factor authentication.
Review login activity.
Report to FBI IC3.

contacts.name: FBI IC3
contacts.when_to_use: Report here if your account was taken over or money was involved

contacts.name: FTC Report Fraud
contacts.when_to_use: Use for broader fraud or identity-related reporting

title: Identity Theft / Personal Data Exposure
description: Someone is using your personal information without permission.
chat_response: I know this can feel overwhelming, but there are clear steps that can help you recover and protect yourself.
steps: Start recovery at IdentityTheft.gov.
Place fraud alerts on credit reports.
Monitor financial accounts closely.

contacts.name: IdentityTheft.gov
contacts.when_to_use: Use immediately to begin identity recovery steps

contacts.name: FBI IC3
contacts.when_to_use: Use if the identity theft involved online crime or financial loss

title: Ransomware / Malware Infection
description: Software that locks your files/system and demands payment.
chat_response: This is a serious situation, but paying the ransom usually makes things worse. Reporting quickly is the right move.
steps: Disconnect device from the internet.
Do NOT pay the ransom.
Report to CISA and FBI IC3.
Preserve evidence if possible.

contacts.name: CISA Incident Reporting
contacts.when_to_use: Use for guidance and reporting ransomware incidents

contacts.name: FBI IC3
contacts.when_to_use: Use to report ransomware or financial loss

title: Personal Images Leak / Sextortion
description: Threats to share private images unless money is paid.
chat_response: I'm really sorry you're dealing with this. You're not alone, and help is available. The most important thing is not to engage or pay.
steps: Do NOT pay or respond.
Save evidence (screenshots, messages, usernames).
Report immediately using the contacts below.

contacts.name: FBI Sextortion Help
contacts.when_to_use: Use immediately if someone is threatening to leak images

contacts.name: FBI IC3
contacts.when_to_use: Use if money was demanded or accounts were compromised

contacts.name: NCMEC CyberTipline
contacts.description: National Center for Missing & Exploited Children
contacts.when_to_use: MANDATORY if a minor is involved

emergency_notice: Do not pay. It usually leads to more demands.

title: Poisoning / Overdose / Toxic Exposure
description: Possible poisoning, medication overdose, or chemical exposure.
chat_response: If someone is unconscious, not breathing, seizing, or in immediate danger, call 911 now. Otherwise, Poison Control can guide you immediately.
steps: Call Poison Control immediately for guidance.
Have the substance container ready if possible.
Call 911 if unconscious, not breathing, or seizing.

contacts.name: Poison Control
contacts.when_to_use: Immediate poisoning/exposure guidance when not already in critical distress

contacts.name: Emergency (911)
contacts.when_to_use: If symptoms are severe (unconscious, not breathing, seizures)

emergency_notice: Call 911 if unconscious, not breathing, or having seizures.

title: Report Suspected Abuse or Neglect
description: Reporting suspected abuse or neglect of a child or vulnerable adult.
chat_response: You can and should report suspected abuse or neglect to protect the person at risk.
steps: If anyone is in immediate danger, call 911 right away.
If not immediate emergency, contact the abuse reporting hotline.
You do not need proof; report what you have observed.
Follow any instructions given by hotline staff.

contacts.name: DSS Abuse Reporting
contacts.when_to_use: Report suspected child or vulnerable adult abuse/neglect

contacts.name: Emergency (911)
contacts.when_to_use: If anyone is in immediate danger

emergency_notice: Call 911 if in immediate danger. Reporting in good faith is protected.

title: Disaster / Severe Weather / Emergency Management
description: County emergency management and disaster-related resources.
chat_response: For immediate life-threatening emergencies, call 911. For county preparedness resources and emergency management info, you can contact the Office of Emergency Management.
steps: For immediate danger (injury, trapped persons), call 911.
For preparedness info and county emergency management, contact OEM.
Sign up for emergency alerts.

contacts.name: Office of Emergency Management
contacts.when_to_use: Preparedness info, disaster guidance, county emergency resources

contacts.name: Emergency (911)
contacts.when_to_use: Immediate danger (injury, trapped persons, fire)

title: Eviction or Rent Assistance
description: Behind on rent or facing eviction - emergency assistance and legal help.
chat_response: There are local agencies that may help with emergency rent, housing stability, and legal questions about eviction.
steps: Gather key documents: lease, eviction notices, ID, proof of income.
Contact DSS for emergency eviction prevention and rental assistance.
Contact legal aid for housing/landlord-tenant legal questions.
If you have a court date, reach out to legal aid ASAP.

contacts.name: Department of Social Services
contacts.when_to_use: Ask about emergency eviction prevention programs

emergency_notice: Do not ignore court notices. Seek legal aid immediately if you have a court date.
